question_text:
Valid Palindrome II

Given a string s, return true if the s can be a palindrome after deleting at most one character from it.
output_format: Print "true" if it can be a palindrome after at most one deletion, else "false".
constraints: s consists of lowercase English letters.
explanation: We use two pointers converging from the ends. When `s[left] != s[right]`, we have one chance to skip either the left or the right character. We check if either `s[left+1..right]` or `s[left..right-1]` is a palindrome. If neither is, return false. If the pointers converge without mismatch, the string is already a palindrome. This runs in O(N) time and O(1) space because the inner palindrome check is at most one full pass.
